prerequisites: Account Owner access, which is required to locate and retrieve the API access token.
An access token (bearer token) obtained from your Float Account Settings Integrations page.
A start date to bound the historical window the connector reads from.

authMethods.name: Access token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Float authenticates with bearer tokens. Supply the access_token value, which the connector uses as the bearer token for authentication. The token is obtained from your Float Account Settings Integrations page, and only an Account Owner can locate it.

setupSteps.title: Retrieve your Float access token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Float as an Account Owner and open Account Settings. Go to the Integrations page and copy the API access token shown there. This is the bearer token Float uses for authentication.

setupSteps.title: Provide the access_token credential
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied token as the access_token field. The connector uses it as a bearer token for authentication on every request. The Float API returns JSON by default when no Accept header is sent, and you can set the Accept header to application/json to request JSON explicitly.

setupSteps.title: Set the start_date
setupSteps.body: Provide the required start_date to define the earliest point from which the connector replicates Float data.
